# CWE-924 negative: HMAC-verified webhook payload (pure stdlib)
lang: go
file: CWE-924-safe.pure.go
variant: pure-go
---
package sample

// CWE-924 mitigated: reject webhook when HMAC signature does not match body.

import (
	"crypto/hmac"
	"crypto/sha256"
	"database/sql"
	"encoding/hex"
	"encoding/json"
	"io"
	"net/http"
)

type PaymentEventVerifiedPure struct {
	InvoiceID string  `json:"invoice_id"`
	Amount    float64 `json:"amount"`
}

func AcceptWebhookVerifiedPure(db *sql.DB, secret []byte) http.HandlerFunc {
	return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		body, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)
		if err != nil {
			http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusBadRequest)
			return
		}
		sigHeader := r.Header.Get("X-Signature")
		mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, secret)
		mac.Write(body)
		expected := hex.EncodeToString(mac.Sum(nil))
		if !hmac.Equal([]byte(expected), []byte(sigHeader)) {
			http.Error(w, "invalid signature", http.StatusUnauthorized)
			return
		}
		var evt PaymentEventVerifiedPure
		if err := json.Unmarshal(body, &evt); err != nil {
			http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusBadRequest)
			return
		}
		_, err = db.Exec(
			"UPDATE invoices SET paid = true, amount = $1 WHERE id = $2",
			evt.Amount, evt.InvoiceID,
		)
		if err != nil {
			http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)
			return
		}
		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
		w.Write([]byte(`{"status":"applied"}`))
	}
}
